mission.title: linkerd: Prometheus-Operator PodMonitor Support
mission.description: Prometheus-Operator PodMonitor Support. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current linkerd deployment
steps.description:
Verify your linkerd version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
helm list -n linkerd 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working linkerd installation.

steps.title: Review linkerd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant linkerd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
kubectl get configmap -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=linkerd
```
## Feature Request

### What problem are you trying to solve?

I have powerful Prometheus setup deployed with prometheus-operator, extended with Thanos.
I want to bring my own Prometheus without changing prometheus config.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Prometheus-Operator PodMonitor Support
steps.description:
Add PodMonitor resources to the Helm chart

With an external Prometheus setup installed using prometheus-operator the Prometheus instance scraping can be configured using Service/PodMonitor resources.

By adding PodMonitor resource into Linkerd Helm chart we can mimic the configuration of bundled
```yaml
Subject

Problem

Solution

Validation

Fixes #[GitHub issue ID]

DCO Sign off
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
kubectl get events -n linkerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Prometheus-Operator PodMonitor Support" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Add PodMonitor resources to the Helm chart

With an external Prometheus setup installed using prometheus-operator the Prometheus instance scraping can be configured using Service/PodMonitor resources.
codeSnippets:
Subject

Problem

Solution

Validation

Fixes #[GitHub issue ID]

DCO Sign off

Introduce Pull Request Template

GitHub's community guidelines recommend a pull request template, the repo was
lacking one.

Introduce a `PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` file.

Once merged, the
[Community profile checklist](https://github.com/linkerd/linkerd2/community)
should indicate the repo now provides a pull request template.

Fixes #3321

Signed-off-by: Jane Smith <jane.smith@example.com>

# linkerd-proxy-podmonitor.yaml
---
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: PodMonitor
metadata:
  name: linkerd-proxy
  namespace: linkerd-viz
spec:
  podTargetLabels:
  - "linkerd.io/proxy-deployment"  # pass proxy-deployment label for additional info (optional)
  podMetricsEndpoints:               # tell prometheus which port/path to scrape
  - interval: 30s
    path: /metrics
    port: linkerd-admin
  jobLabel: linkerd-sidecar
  selector: 
    matchExpressions:       # label selector match all injected pods
    - key: linkerd.io/control-plane-ns
      operator: Exists
  namespaceSelector:
    any: true  # search pods in all namespaces

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with linkerd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
